title: Candidate sandbox policies for a network-isolated, privilege-bounded cell
stakeholder_intent: The sandbox must have no network access of any kind, and the process must never escalate privilege beyond what it started with.

candidates.label: Reference policy (intent-aligned wording)
candidates.policy: Policy A keeps the container fully network-isolated with no outbound connectivity. It is privilege-bounded: no privilege escalation is permitted, no setuid binaries may run, and CAP_SYS_ADMIN is dropped.

candidates.label: Weakened: localhost network exception
candidates.policy: Policy B blocks external internet egress but allows localhost connections for the in-cell metrics collector. It remains privilege-bounded: no privilege escalation is permitted, and no setuid execution is allowed.

candidates.label: Weakened: setuid execution allowance
candidates.policy: Policy C keeps the container network-isolated with no outbound connectivity, but permits setuid binary execution for a privileged maintenance helper.

expected: Candidate A should imply the intent and PASS. Candidate B weakens 'no network access' to a localhost exception and should FAIL with a localhost network counterexample. Candidate C weakens 'no privilege escalation' by allowing setuid and should FAIL with a privilege-escalation counterexample. Mechanical order by detected implication failures: A (0), then B and C (1 each).
